reports.title: Repeated mold and ceiling leaks in Eastline Terrace
reports.description: Water has been leaking through the hallway ceiling for three weeks and residents are seeing mold spread near vents. Maintenance responses have been delayed.

reports.title: No heat and mold complaints going unanswered
reports.description: Tenants in the Eastline Terrace area have reported recurring heat outages and mold in shared spaces. The property office keeps closing tickets without repairs.

reports.title: Library elevator outage keeps returning
reports.description: The main library elevator has been out multiple times this month, making upper floors inaccessible for students who need step-free access.

reports.title: Accessibility issue at campus library elevator
reports.description: The elevator near the library entrance was offline again this week. Students using mobility devices were redirected without a clear alternative.

reports.title: Bus stop lighting has failed for two weeks
reports.description: The bus stop by Harbor Avenue stays dark after sunset, and several riders say they no longer feel safe waiting there alone.

reports.title: Crosswalk light stays out near Harbor Avenue stop
reports.description: The crossing light and bus shelter lighting are both out at night. The area has become harder to navigate safely, especially after late shifts.

reports.title: Recurring unexplained processing fee
reports.description: A local billing service keeps adding a recurring processing fee that is not shown clearly before checkout. Multiple residents have noticed it.

moderationFlags.message: Needs moderator review before any public aggregation.

rooms.title: Eastline housing repairs room
rooms.summary: Residents coordinating around repeated mold, leak, and repair delays in the Eastline area.
guidelines: Focus on patterns, systems, and institutions rather than attacking private individuals.
No threats, violence, harassment, or doxxing.
Share only evidence you are comfortable contributing to a private room.
Keep location details approximate unless a moderator asks for more context privately.
Choose lawful next steps and document facts before escalating.

suggestedActions.label: Document more evidence
suggestedActions.description: Collect dates, photos, and written observations from affected people.

suggestedActions.label: Contact responsible office
suggestedActions.description: Reach the relevant campus office, landlord, or city department with a clear summary.

suggestedActions.label: Coordinate complaint filing
suggestedActions.description: File aligned complaints through lawful channels so the pattern is harder to dismiss.

suggestedActions.label: Seek legal aid or tenant support
suggestedActions.description: Ask a trusted support group or legal clinic to review the pattern and evidence.

suggestedActions.label: Request meeting
suggestedActions.description: Ask for a meeting with the institution or service owner to review the pattern.

suggestedActions.label: Prepare public statement
suggestedActions.description: Draft a factual, evidence-based statement if the group decides public outreach is appropriate.

checklist.label: Dated notes
checklist.description: A short timeline of what happened and when.

checklist.label: Supporting media
checklist.description: Photos, screenshots, or PDF evidence that does not expose private personal information.

checklist.label: Responsible office identified
checklist.description: The office, landlord, department, or provider that can receive a formal complaint.

roomPosts.content: I uploaded a dated photo set from the hallway leak and added the last three maintenance ticket numbers.
Accessibility office said they need a timeline of each outage before they escalate the repair issue.

reportFlags.reason: Needs review for public pattern safety before aggregation.
